reasoning: The ICE BofA Corporate OAS was at an exceptionally tight 0.76% as of October 30, 2025. While this level is near historical minimums (making eventual mean reversion likely), the current market environment clearly features a strong tightening momentum, likely supported by risk-on sentiment and favorable corporate debt demand. Over the very short term (7 days to Nov 16), it is more likely that spreads will continue their steady grind tighter or remain flat (resulting in a decrease or no change). As the forecast horizon extends to 1 month (Dec 9) and 3 months (Feb 7), the risk of unpredictable macro volatility increases and mean-reverting tendencies strengthen. Consequently, uncertainty rises and the likelihood of an increase moves progressively closer to a coin flip (0.50), reflecting reduced predictability over longer durations.
